narrative:
Mathías Olivera is a Left-Back representing Uruguay at the FIFA World Cup 2026. His overall rating of 7.11/10 places him among the top 6% of left-backs in this tournament. This rating primarily reflects his club form over the last two seasons, which accounts for 66% of his score.

Olivera demonstrates strong defensive engagement, averaging 2.667 tackles and 2.333 clearances per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His attacking output is evident through 1.667 key passes and 0.116 expected assists per 90 minutes. However, his expected goals at this World Cup stand at just 0.016 per 90 minutes, indicating he is not a direct scoring threat.

With 270 minutes played, Olivera is a consistent fixture in Uruguay's defense. His contribution is balanced across defensive duties and offensive support from the flank. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his reliability in regaining possession and his ability to generate attacking opportunities in upcoming matches.
